source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/06_인허가 조사 및 보고자료/230906 - 2G_Aspenstate_Test report 추가발급/3.2 Risk Management Report.docx

document.title: Risk Management Report for A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW

sourceSummary.oneLine: Risk management report for the H&abyz Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Establishes and documents risk management activities, residual risk acceptability, and production and post-production information collection for the medical device.
keyPoints: Revision 1 dated Dec/02/2022 states that EN ISO 14971:2019 was applied.
The device is classified as IIa and has an expected service life of approximately 5 years.
The intended purpose is general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children, not mammography.
Before risk control, 40 risks were identified and estimated; 2 were acceptable and 38 were unacceptable.
After risk control, all identified residual risks were evaluated as acceptable.

Production and post-production information collection includes customer satisfaction, complaints, inspection and test monitoring, design changes, standards monitoring, market information, and adverse event reporting if necessary.

entities.name: H&abyz Co., Ltd.
entities.evidence: This is a document for the H&abyz Co., Ltd. to establish and document a risk management plan in accordance with the risk management procedure.

entities.name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
entities.evidence: Product name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

entities.name: European Union
entities.evidence: Applicable national and regional regulations: European Union - Regulation (EU) 2017/745 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 5 April 2017

entities.name: Republic of Korea
entities.evidence: Applicable national and regional regulations: Republic of Korea - Medical Device Act in Korea

entities.name: IEC 60601-1
entities.evidence: Safety Test according to IEC 60601-1: Test report No. REP001824, REP001824_1

entities.name: IEC 62304
entities.evidence: establishing software development process in accordance with IEC 62304

concepts.name: Risk management plan
concepts.evidence: This plan includes the following: the scope of the planned risk management activities... assignment of responsibilities and authorities... criteria for risk acceptability...

concepts.name: Risk acceptability criteria
concepts.evidence: Acceptable Risk (A): Acceptable Region, Green Zone, Risk levels: 1~4; Unacceptable Risk (U): Unacceptable Region, Red Zone, Risk levels: 5~25

concepts.name: Intended purpose
concepts.evidence: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

concepts.name: Contraindications
concepts.evidence: Not to be used for mammography and direct cardiac application. Not to be used for infant and pregnant women.

concepts.name: Essential performance
concepts.evidence: Manufacturer shall identify which functions are Essential Performance. - Acquisition image is obtained as intended. - The acquired image should not have two lines adjacent or be blank.

concepts.name: Reasonably foreseeable misuse
concepts.evidence: Might be misused by maintenance and calibration error of operator. User misuse may occur depending on the manufacturer's maintenance responsibilities and modes.

concepts.name: Residual risk evaluation
concepts.evidence: All identified residual risks evaluated after implementation of the risk control measures, are distributed in an acceptable region.

concepts.name: Benefit-risk analysis
concepts.evidence: The benefit-risk analysis is used to determine if the residual risk is outweighed by the expected benefits of the intended use of the medical device.

concepts.name: Production and post-production information
concepts.evidence: Refer to A-TCF-5-161 Post-Market Surveillance for getting the information of production and post- production phases.

evidence.claim: The document is a Risk Management Report for A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk Management Report for A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW
evidence.pageTarget: Risk Management Report for A1717MCW A1417MCW F1417MCW

evidence.claim: The product is a Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Product name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: Revision 1 is dated Dec/02/2022 and applied EN ISO 14971:2019.
evidence.evidenceText: Revision No. 1 Rev. Date Dec/02/2022 Revision Description Applied EN ISO 14971:2019

evidence.claim: The applicable jurisdictions listed are the European Union and Republic of Korea.
evidence.evidenceText: Applicable national and regional regulations: European Union - Regulation (EU) 2017/745... Republic of Korea - Medical Device Act in Korea

evidence.claim: The device classification is IIa.
evidence.evidenceText: Classification IIa

evidence.claim: The expected service life is approximately 5 years.
evidence.evidenceText: Expected service life Approximately 5 years

evidence.claim: The intended purpose is general radiographic diagnosis for adults and children, excluding mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children... Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The detector may be wired or wireless and supports auto exposure detection without generator integration.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW are wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors... The detectors support an auto exposure detection technology that allows the detectors to be used without generator integration.

evidence.claim: The product consists of TFT, CsI scintillator, and electronics that convert read data into digital signals.
evidence.evidenceText: The detector consists of TFT and Scintillator (CsI) and an electronic device that converts data read through it into digital signals.

evidence.claim: The device is not intended for mammography, direct cardiac application, infant use, or pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography and direct cardiac application. Not to be used for infant and pregnant women.

evidence.claim: The device has patient contact at the front of the detector for less than 10 minutes.
evidence.evidenceText: Patient contacts the front of the detector in less than 10 minutes.

evidence.claim: The device contains software.
evidence.evidenceText: A.2.19 Does the medical device contain software? A The product contains software.

evidence.claim: The detector can connect to a PC via Wi-Fi, and the power supply box can connect to a PC by Ethernet cable.
evidence.evidenceText: Power Supply Box can be connected to the PC with an Ethernet Cable. The detector can be connected to a PC via Wi-Fi.

evidence.claim: The device does not store data according to the report.
evidence.evidenceText: A.2.21 Does the medical device store data critical to patient care? N/A This product does not store data.

evidence.claim: Risk estimation uses five severity categories and five probability categories.
evidence.evidenceText: Severity Category... Catastrophic 5... Negligible 1; Probability of occurrence Category... Frequent 5... Improbable 1

evidence.claim: Acceptable risk levels are 1 to 4 and unacceptable risk levels are 5 to 25.
evidence.evidenceText: Acceptable Risk (A): Acceptable Region, Green Zone, Risk levels: 1~4; Unacceptable Risk (U): Unacceptable Region, Red Zone, Risk levels: 5~25

evidence.claim: Before risk control, 40 risks were identified and estimated, with 2 acceptable and 38 unacceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Through the identification of known and foreseeable hazards, 40 risks were identified and estimated... Acceptable region: 2ea - Unacceptable region: 38ea

evidence.claim: Risk reduction was required for risks in the Red Zone.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk reduction is required for the risks that belong to the Red Zone.

evidence.claim: Risk control options included inherently safe design, protective measures, information for safety, and user training where appropriate.
evidence.evidenceText: We used one or more of the following risk control options... inherently safe design and manufacture... protective measures in the medical device itself or in the manufacturing process... information for safety and, where appropriate, training to users

evidence.claim: After risk control, all identified residual risks were evaluated in an acceptable region.
evidence.evidenceText: All identified residual risks evaluated after implementation of the risk control measures, are distributed in an acceptable region.

evidence.claim: Overall residual risks were determined acceptable through warning, operating method, adverse event reporting, and usability expert review.
evidence.evidenceText: Finally, the overall residual risks are determined to be acceptable through the warning, operating method and adverse event reporting and usability expert review.

evidence.claim: Production and post-production information is obtained through Post-Market Surveillance document A-TCF-5-161.
evidence.evidenceText: Refer to A-TCF-5-161 Post-Market Surveillance for getting the information of production and post- production phases.

evidence.claim: The Quality manager is responsible to collect, review information, and manage quality problems in production and post-production phase.
evidence.evidenceText: Responsibility and the role of the Quality manager Collect, review the information and manage the quality problems in production and post-production phase

evidence.claim: Production and post-production risk analysis is performed once every 12 months.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk analysis for the collected information (once every 12 months)

evidence.claim: The Quality manager reports safety information and side effects to the regulatory agency if necessary.
evidence.evidenceText: According to the seriousness of the information collected, the Quality manager shall report safety information and any side effects to the regulatory agency (Applicable national or regional regulations).

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the source text identifies European Union and Republic of Korea regulations and does not provide FDA authority evidence.
Conflicting numeric evidence: after risk control table states EN ISO 14971:2019 has 40 acceptable, 0 unacceptable, total 38, while surrounding text says all identified residual risks are acceptable.
Potential OCR or transcription quality issues are present, including misspellings such as 'corraective', 'vaildation', and inconsistent references to EN 15322-1 versus listed EN 15223-1.
